title: aeschynomene sensitiva var. hispidula
intro: Aeschynomene sensitiva var. hispidula, also known as sensitive plant or touch-me-not, is a plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's medicinal properties include its use in traditional remedies to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain, improve wound healing, and combat infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Aeschynomene sensitiva var. hispidula are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
